2606.25462.title: Optimizing Abstractive Summarization With Fine-Tuned PEGASUS
2606.25462.paper_summary: Abstractive text summarization is the technique of generating a short and concise summary comprising the salient ideas of a source text without making a subset of the salient sentences from the source text. The introduction of transformer models such as BART, T5, and PEGASUS has made this sort of summarization process more efficient and accurate. The objective of this paper is to fine-tune PEGASUS on the XL-Sum English corpus to achieve a better performance compared to the baseline mT5 model. The performance of the generated summaries from the fine-tuned model is evaluated using the ROUGE metric, which basically compares the auto-generated summaries with human-created summaries. To the best of our knowledge, the results from our fine-tuned PEGASUS model give a state-of-the-art performance on the XL-Sum English Corpus. To quantify the improvement, there is a 4.04% improvement in the ROUGE-1 score, a 15.25% increase in the ROUGE-2 score, and a 3.39% improvement in the ROUGE-L score from the baseline model.
2606.25462.paper_summary_zh: 抽象文本摘要是生成一個簡短且精煉的摘要的技術，該摘要包含來源文本的主要思想，而不是從來源文本中提取出一部分突出的句子。像 BART、T5 和 PEGASUS 等Transformer模型的引入使這種摘要過程變得更加高效和準確。本文的目標是對 XL-Sum 英文語料庫上的 PEGASUS 進行微調，以達到比基線 mT5 模型更好的性能。從微調模型生成的摘要的性能是使用 ROUGE 指標進行評估的，該指標基本上是將自動生成的摘要與人類創建的摘要進行比較。據我們所知，我們微調的 PEGASUS 模型在 XL-Sum 英文語料庫上達到了最先進的性能。為了量化改進，ROUGE-1 分數提高了 4.04%，ROUGE-2 分數增加了 15.25%，ROUGE-L 分數從基線模型提高了 3.39%。
2606.25462.author: Sadiul Arefin Rafi et.al.
2606.25462.authors: Sadiul Arefin Rafi, Naimur Rahman, Kazi Nazibul Islam, Ha-mim Ahmad, Farig Yousuf Sadeque
